document.title: Risk Management Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report A-RMR-0-01 Rev.4 for HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management documentation for the medical device, including risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information.
keyPoints: The report states risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
The expected service life of HAD1717MC(MG) is approximately 5 years.
Residual risks after control measures are evaluated as acceptable.
Incident feedback reports zero MFDS, FDA, and MHRA report cases for 2021 through 2024.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: sourcePath references India CDSCO, source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, and the document text references MFDS, FDA, MHRA, CE, KFDA, and Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
Document date evidence conflicts: header and approvals show Nov 20, 2024 for Rev.4, but the conclusion section shows Date: February 28, 2023.
No issuing regulatory authority is identified in the document body; the report appears to be manufacturer-generated.
OCR/text quality includes typographical errors such as 'Risk(LVEL)' and 'Not Acceptable Zoon', but key metadata remains readable.
